Coding Assignment #1

Clayton Jones

2025-07-11

Scenario 1: Exit Velocity by Pitch

Create a visual that summarizes the exit velocity separated by pitch type and grouped by the 2023 and 2024 seasons. The plot will be like Figure 4.16 but will include a separate density plot for each year within a pitch type. Be sure that you have a proper title, x and y axis labels, and the full name for each pitch as opposed to the abbreviations (i.e., “Four-seam Fastball” as opposed to FF).

Average Exit Velocity by Year
Pitch 2023 Average Exit Velocity (mph) 2024 Average Exit Velocity (mph) Difference
Sweeper 84.42 96.19 11.77
Knuckle Curve 86.42 93.55 7.13
Curveball 92.64 88.11 4.53
Cutter 96.40 93.13 3.26
4-Seam Fastball 97.25 94.19 3.06
Changeup 90.41 88.27 2.14
Split-Finger 93.47 92.23 1.24
Sinker 94.49 93.86 0.63
Slider 91.35 90.78 0.57
Eephus 97.20 NA NA
Slurve 95.90 NA NA
Pitch Counts
Pitch Number of Times Seen
4-Seam Fastball 1097
Slider 616
Changeup 551
Sinker 531
Cutter 366
Curveball 271
Sweeper 231
Split-Finger 212
85
Knuckle Curve 81
Slurve 16
Other 6
Eephus 2
Forkball 2
Knuckleball 1

Question 1: Which pitch saw the largest difference in average exit velocities between years? What was the difference?
As shown in Table 1, the biggest difference in average exit velocity between years was the sweeper, with an increase of 12.6 mph in 2024.

Question 2: Which pitch was seen most often in 2023 and 2024?
As shown in Table 2, the four seam fastball was seen most often, with a total of 1099 pitches.

Scenario 2: Exit Velocity and Launch Angle

Create a table that summarizes the average exit velocity, average launch angle, and frequency of occurrence for each unique event for both 2023 and 2024. Ensure that you have a proper title.

Average Exit Velocity, Average Launch Angle, and Frequency of Occurence by Year
Event Average Exit Velocity (mph) Average Launch Angle Frequency
double 101.71739 17.3333333 69
double_play 106.65000 8.0000000 2
field_error 82.20000 0.3333333 3
field_out 90.36716 15.5021186 472
fielders_choice 74.50000 -13.5000000 2
force_out 86.00667 -7.6666667 15
grounded_into_double_play 89.16471 -6.6470588 17
home_run 105.48696 28.5362319 69
sac_fly 95.06250 29.8750000 8
single 91.77485 4.7602339 171

Question 1: Which event occurred most often?
As shown in Table 2, the most common event was a field_out, which is a ball put into play where Seager (the batter) gets out. This includes fly outs, pop outs, ground outs, lineouts.

Question 2: Which event had the highest average exit velocity?
Surprisingly, according to Table 2, the highest average exit velocity was on double plays, not home runs. Statcast splits ‘double plays’ and ‘grounded into double plays’, which means that these events were likely line outs that were hit so hard that another runner was doubled off. This type of play can usually only occur when the ball is hit hard, which would make sense why it was the hardest. It only happened twice over two seasons. Among more common events, home runs had the highest average exit velocities, which would make the most sense.

Create a visual like Figure 4.23 that summarizes the density of exit velocities for 2023 and 2024. Be sure that you have a proper title, x and y axis labels, and a legend.

Scenario 3: Swing Probabilities

Create a visual like 4.28 that summarizes all hit-by-pitch for 2023 and 2024. Be sure that you have a proper title and x and y axis labels.

Create two visuals (like the one on pg. 72) that produce a heat map summarizing swing probabilities for the 2023 and 2024 seasons separately. be sure that you have a proper title and x and y axis labels for each plot.

Question 1: Discuss at least one key difference between the two years.
Looking at the two plots in Figure 4, if the pitch is in the strike zone, Seager is going to swing. Seager is known as an aggressive hitter, and these plots prove it. There is not a lot of difference between the two years. In 2024, Seager expanded below the zone and swung at pitchers below the strikezone a little bit more. Beyond that, there is hardly any difference between the two years.

Create three visuals that summarize swing probabilities for Fastballs (FF, FT), Breaking Balls (CU, FC, SL), and Off-speed pitches (CH, FO, FS, SI, KN, KC, GY) combined for both 2023 and 2024. Be sure that you have a proper title and x and y axis labels for each plot. Include the total number of pitches seen for each group in the title.

Question 1: Discuss differences across all three plots.
Looking at the plots in Figure 4, as with the years, there is not a ton of difference between pitches, specifically between breaking balls and off-speed pitches. Seager does not chase fastballs below the zone like he does with off-speed and breaking balls, but will swing at fastballs above the zone. If Seager is going to chase. below the zone, it will more likely be for a breaking ball than an off-speed pitch. Overall, Seager will swing at anything in the zone, and will expand the zone for high breaking balls and low breaking balls and off-speed pitches.
